steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The question asks what "carves" and "leaves" both mean in context. Gogol carves his name into clay (making a permanent impression) and leaves his signature in brush strokes on paintings (making a visible trace). Both actions create a lasting mark of his identity.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A. Marks means makes a visible sign or impression on a surface. Substituting: "he marks his name into the bottoms of clay cups" and "marks his signature in fat brush strokes" — both work perfectly. A is correct.

B. Passes means moves by or transfers something. Gogol is not moving past the clay or transferring the paintings — he is creating impressions on them. Eliminated.

C. Turns means rotates or changes direction. Neither carving a name into clay nor brushing a signature involves turning anything. Eliminated.

D. Follows means goes after or traces a path set by something else. Gogol is not following anything — he is originating the marks himself. Eliminated.
